root_text.sn52.11:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā anuruddho sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn52.11:1.2: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yenāyasmā anuruddho tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā anuruddhena saddhiṁ …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ anuruddhaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn52.11:2.1: “Katamesaṁ āyasmā anuruddho dhammānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto”ti?
root_text.sn52.11:2.2: “Catunnaṁ khvāhaṁ, āvuso, satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto.
root_text.sn52.11:2.7: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharāmi ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ—
root_text.sn52.11:2.8: imesaṁ khvāhaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā mahābhiññataṁ patto.
root_text.sn52.11:2.9: Imesañca panāhaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ satipaṭṭhānānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā kappasahassaṁ anussarāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn52.11:1.1: At one time Venerable Anuruddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn52.11:1.2: Then several mendicants went up to Venerable Anuruddha, exchanged greetings with him … and said:
translation_text.sn52.11:2.1: “What things has Venerable Anuruddha developed and cultivated to attain great direct knowledge?”
translation_text.sn52.11:2.2: “Reverends, I attained great direct knowledge by developing and cultivating the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn52.11:2.4: I meditate observing an aspect of the body …
translation_text.sn52.11:2.7: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn52.11:2.8: I attained great direct knowledge by developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn52.11:2.9: And it’s because of developing and cultivating these four kinds of mindfulness meditation that I recollect a thousand eons.”
